name: Estate and Daily-Life Infrastructure by Polity
summary: Operational estate logic translating polity identity into residence, governance, archive, ceremony, logistics, and staff structures usable at table scale.
global_rule: Estates are not generic fantasy manors; each polity expresses wealth, governance, daily logistics, and household power through setting-native infrastructure.

estate_types.Shelk: aristocratic residence plus governance plus ceremony plus archives plus cultural patronage
estate_types.Roaz: citadel-estate plus judiciary plus enforcement plus engineering
estate_types.Mimolot: academy-estate plus research plus archive plus magical training
estate_types.Panim: temple-estate plus funerary commerce plus divine mediation
estate_types.Cosmouth: harbor-palace plus archive plus shipwright estate
estate_types.The Union: guild-seat residences rather than hereditary estates
estate_types.Soreheim: communal tower tiers, fortified work settlements, and steward compounds rather than private manors
estate_types.Sheresh: dome-quarter, laboratory, and archive-vault residences rather than landed estates

Imperial Court.status_logic: The nobility expresses status through winged court households, vault control, ceremonial hosting, and access to tribute, records, and reception space.

Shelk.estate_pattern: Residence-governance-ceremony-archive compound embedded in elite urban fabric
Shelk.status_logic: Wealth appears through etiquette control, gardens, curated artifacts, tailor networks, reception culture, and patronage over art and cloth.

Roaz.estate_pattern: Citadel-estate fused with judiciary, security, and industrial command
Roaz.status_logic: Authority is displayed through controlled access, command chambers, prison-security logic, industrial oversight, and documentary control.

Mimolot.estate_pattern: Scholastic estate built around academy, archive, and magical discipline
Mimolot.status_logic: Prestige comes from archive access, relic custody, school authority, ward control, and patronage over students and scholarship.

Panim.estate_pattern: Temple-estate centered on funerary mediation and sacred administration
Panim.status_logic: Wealth and dignity are expressed through sacred custody, funerary quality, ritual order, and mediated access to holy space.

Zootia.estate_pattern: Agrarian production estate with barns, granaries, mills, and transport yards
Zootia.status_logic: Status follows field control, herd size, mill throughput, grain reserve security, and distribution leverage.

Shirsh.estate_pattern: Investigative-magistral estate combining magical inquiry, evidence control, and protected witness handling
Shirsh.status_logic: Authority appears through restricted knowledge, evidence custody, sealed passages, and the management of what may be seen or spoken.

Cosmouth.estate_pattern: Maritime-archival estate with harbor, treasury, shipwright, and retreat variants
Cosmouth.status_logic: Power moves through ledgers, tides, timber, customs rights, admiralty access, and curated noble leisure.

The Union.estate_pattern: Guild-seat residences attached to institutional headquarters rather than hereditary manor grounds
The Union.status_logic: Wealth is displayed through office control, warehouse rights, negotiation suites, contract access, and patronage over workshops.

Soreheim.estate_pattern: Communal tower tiers and fortified work settlements instead of private manors
Soreheim.status_logic: Prestige follows contribution command, logistics oversight, relic custody, production direction, and stewardship over communal survival systems.

Sheresh.estate_pattern: Arcane dome commune and archive-vault quarters integrated with survival infrastructure
Sheresh.status_logic: Influence appears through ward control, research access, powerstone refinement, dome safety, and protected terrace or laboratory space.

support_rule: Estate, manor, guild-seat, dome-quarter, citadel, and tower-tier assets must remain represented as self-sufficient locality-support structures inside the package.
